Spanish tennis player Carlos Alcaraz during a press conference at the Argentina Open in Buenos Aires.

PHOTO: AFP

PublishedFeb 13, 2024, 08:27 AM

UpdatedFeb 13, 2024, 08:34 AM

BUENOS AIRES - Carlos Alcaraz said on Feb 12 if he had to choose between an Olympic gold at the 2024 Paris Olympics or the French Open title he would go for the gold.

The 20-year-old who won the US Open in 2022 and Wimbledon in 2023 was asked about his season goals ahead of the Argentina Open in Buenos Aires where he is defending champion.

“If I had to choose one target this year I’d choose the Olympic gold over Roland-Garros,” the Spaniard said.

The French Open will be played from May 20 to June 9 with the Olympic tennis at the same Roland Garros venue from July 27 to August 4.

“I’m on good form and I played well in Australia. And the tennis I’ve played in training on clay courts has been very good too,” Alcaraz said.AFP

title: Carlos Alcaraz puts Paris Olympics gold above French Open title | The Straits Times
description: The 20-year-old has two Grand Slam titles - the US Open in 2022 and Wimbledon in 2023. Read more at straitstimes.com.
